compiled_state.title: RED: Write failing test for reciprocal LINK event dedup in same sync batch
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
The local ticket system writes reciprocal LINK events for relates_to (A->B and B->A). If both appear in the same git diff, the handler could create duplicate Jira links since Relates is symmetric. The handler needs in-run pair tracking using frozenset({source_jira_key, target_jira_key}).

## Test Approach
Given: Two LINK events in the same batch — one in ticket A dir (target=B, relation=relates_to) and one in ticket B dir (target=A, relation=relates_to), both tickets have SYNC events
When: process_outbound() is called with both events
Then: set_relationship() is called exactly ONCE (not twice)

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py (Edit: add reciprocal dedup test)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test verifies reciprocal LINK events result in only one Jira link creation
  Verify: grep -q "reciprocal\|frozenset\|symmetric" tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py
- [ ] Tests fail (RED — handler does not exist yet)
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "reciprocal" --tb=short -q 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED\|ERROR"
